vocab.meaning: thức khuya để học bài hoặc làm việc
vocab.example: With the finals approaching, I’ve had to burn the midnight oil almost every night this week.

vocab.meaning: bắt đầu học tập một cách nghiêm túc
vocab.example: I usually spend about six hours hitting the books to ensure I grasp all the complex theories.
I don't like to pull an all-nighter, so I try to distribute my study hours evenly throughout the day.

vocab.word: put one's shoulder to the wheel
vocab.meaning: nỗ lực hết sức, làm việc/học tập chăm chỉ
vocab.example: If I want to ace the IELTS, I really need to put my shoulder to the wheel for at least four hours daily.

vocab.word: keep one's nose to the grindstone
vocab.meaning: làm việc/học tập bền bỉ, không ngừng nghỉ
vocab.example: I’ve been keeping my nose to the grindstone lately, spending most of my waking hours in the library.
By dedicating five hours a day to practice, my English skills have improved by leaps and bounds.

vocab.meaning: nỗ lực nhiều hơn mức mong đợi
vocab.example: I often go the extra mile by researching additional materials after my formal classes end.

vocab.meaning: vào lúc sáng sớm tinh mơ
vocab.example: I prefer to start my revision at the crack of dawn when my mind is at its sharpest.

vocab.meaning: tập trung và dồn hết tâm trí vào cái gì
vocab.example: I find it easy to spend hours sinking my teeth into a fascinating research paper.
Spending time on my studies daily is exhausting, but I know it will pay off in the long run.
